pass1_value.note: API keys are passed as Bearer tokens in the Authorization header. Docs mention contacting support for OAuth support.

final_value.note: The API reference states to authenticate by including the API key in the Authorization header as a Bearer token (example shown as `Authorization: Bearer fc-xxxxxxxx`).

pass1_value.note: You can create API keys in the dashboard under Settings → API Keys. Scopes may be limited by plan.

final_value.note: Firecrawl publishes a self-serve "Free" plan on its Pricing page. API keys are managed via the Firecrawl dashboard (Settings → API Keys) per the API reference introduction.

pass1_value.note: API keys are available via the dashboard, but some endpoints require elevated scopes available on paid plans.

final_value.note: The API is buildable after self-serve signup by using an API key from the dashboard (Settings → API Keys). Product limits are plan-based (e.g., rate limits/usage by plan), but the docs do not substantiate the prior claim about paid-plan-only endpoint scopes.
